item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea behind it was that they first noticed slight imperfections (like gas particles not being completely blocked). They then test it with energy forms and discover some more strangeness (which perhaps should be in the article but I felt it was a bit boring to include, and just left the experiment number as a clue)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand. The reflected world is supposed to be identical in every way except chirality. That's the entire premise of this whole thing. So how was it even possible for the reflection to have a "weaker" flamethrower than the "real" one?

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Thank you kindly! :)
Congratulations on the +100! Curiously this is the second skip I upvote into +100 in the last hour. Both were equally deserving of praise. Well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to like this, but the tone is just so unclinical in a few places that it's distracting. Mainly, there's an "our" in the Description and the log of what happened says "snapped back into place unnaturally quickly". There are several instances like that scattered throughout.

item.title: Thernocouple
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP. I am trying to imagine this flamethrow thermocouple and having a hard time understanding the results.

+ show block
– hide block

"A thermocouple was placed in front of SCP-2645.

The jet of flame produced was able to pass through SCP-2645 and another jet of flame was emitted from its surface. The flames emitted appeared identical to those seen in the reflection. However, the temperature of the flame emitted by SCP-2645 was far lower than anticipated.

Analysis of footage revealed that the measurement appearing on the reflected version of the thermocouple was at the expected temperature, not matching its counterpart in reality."

So when I imagine this I see two identical people on opposite sides of a door (basically how this interacts) from the entrance. There is a thermocouple in front of the mirror/entrance. It's not specified how close it is but I don't think it should matter too much since no mention of time/distance is given when noting the temperature showing on the thermocouple. Let's say it's right in front of the mirror/enterance. D is told to shoot dead center. The flames are combining with each other as they pass through the mirror/entrance. There would be a split second the thermocouple could display the temperature of only it's sides flame but once they combine the heat should be that of the hotter flame on both sides. If the thermocouple was closer to the flame thrower than there would be more time to see the difference but again when they combine it will be the same temperature. The above text seems to state the flames did not touch each other and passed through one another which is not possible in how this SCP is stated to work. If it was a short burst it should be noted but again the flames would touch and combine when passing through one another so I would expect the temperature to show the same or similar temperatures. I think this is more a endothermic and exothermic thing where the cooler flame gets hotter and the hotter flame gets cooler. The reason I think it would show the hotter temperature is a flamethrow is a stream of fire and could easily be 3+ meters long (I imagine D could not react quick since they cannot see the flame from the mirror coming towards them as it's masked by their own flame so a constant stream is expected) touching both thermocouples at the same time.

I like the SCP but that just threw me out of it. I am probably just not understanding it as I was near certain I would find clarification in the comments but found none. I actually made this account to ask this.


Please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

If I am right and it should be a combined flame temperature showing I suggest stating the thermocouple was in front of the flame thrower and before the flame could reach the opposite thermocouple the temperature was much lower on the SCP side. I still have trouble imaging that with the whole endothermic exothermic area in my mind and a constant stream of fire. I am just trying to imagine what happens when a flame meets another flame and I could have sworn they basically just combine into one (like two birthday candles next to each other, the flame is between them as they are drawn to one another). In that case I think they would still be burned equally bad since really the flame is coming right at the thrower nozzle and that is still proceeding towards D. I am probably just not getting it so any clarification would help.

Thanks for the SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great execution of an old concept. Near the end I though there'd be the twist of "The reflections are just nice dudes that copy us" but it took strange and creepy turn when they substituted the agent with a look alike.

Well earned +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the way it starts out as a pretty simple expression of a thought we've all had about mirrors, and then veers sharply into WTF and stays there for several paragraphs, without actually introducing any new anomalous elements. Everything that happens in the experiment log is organically derived from the original innocuous anomaly, and nonetheless manages to build into something utterly outrageous that leaves you with so many questions, even though you felt like you totally understood what was going on back in the early description. That's brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps it's not clear enough, but you know what happened to her… eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to know where the guy went, and what happened to him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, but I think the incident at the end is unnecessary. The thing controlling this retconned the reality behind the mirror easily, why can't it do the same to ours? Why would this thing let us know that it put in a corpse? Where did the D-class go after escaping nto the mirror?

Also, full-size doesn't say much to me, exact measurements would be better.

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reason why D-5461 was able to step through is because his double was killed by the flamethrower. You'll notice that the article mentions earlier that D-5461's burnt remains were observed on the other side of the mirror.

item.title: Er, you did notice . . .
item.content:encoded:
… what changed between the two times, right?

+ show block
– hide block

D-5461's reflection died, since the "real" version had a more powerful flamethrower. As noted in the article, this left no reflection to block the way upon their return to the mirror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I'll come back to this some time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly thought so. Having it hang the way it does now leaves the article without some effective closure, IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A couple of people suggested that it felt a little unnecessary and felt a bit of an anti-climactic way to end the piece. Do you think it was better before I cut it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … what happened to the Audio log you had in the draft?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ah, so the mirror entities aren't just a parellel universe thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the mirror or reflection entities fixing reality to match the mirror version, thereby correcting the mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this but didn't quite get the ending

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one isn't really doing it for me.

+ mah issues
– hide block

You seem to set up this thing that follows these rules

Any object that would come into contact with SCP-2645's surface does not touch glass, but in fact encounters an exact duplicate of itself projected from an unknown location… It is therefore hypothesized that SCP-2645 is some form of extra-dimensional window or opening that mimics the properties of a mirror.

and then very quickly break the rules:

D-5461 was instructed to touch SCP-2645, but did not come into contact with any obstruction, her hand instead passing through the mirror's surface. Ignoring orders, D-5461 stepped through SCP-2645.

Yes, it was hinted (and eventually shown thoroughly) that what happens on the other side doesn't always line up with what happens on our side, but wouldn't the description be revised as soon as that was discovered?

Overall, the tone and feel of the article just felt messy. I don't have anything specific to say about that, it just didn't feel good reading it.

-1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, should be a pretty minor change. I shall add the light tube system and back up generator when I'm feeling a little less frazzled.

EDIT: Took me longer than expected to get around to it, but I've made these changes now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You might specify the skylight as a light tube system- those collect light from outside, and pipe it through an internally-reflective tube to the interior of a building. They have the advantage of being able to use lenses to concentrate the light from a broad collection area into a single point- sort of like a lighthouse in reverse, or a telescope that doesn't create an image. A backup generator would still be a good idea, but passive lighting doesn't run out of fuel and works as long as it's not a new moon on a cloudy night.

Actually use them in prisons for the above reasons, and because you can't break out through a fiber optic cable attached to the center of a collection dish inside a one-way mirror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you- addressing your queries:

It's unclear what you mean by "expected".

I meant it matched the temperature that they'd expect the flames to be at.

Surely an independent generator is preferable to putting it in a room with a skylight? 1, because skylights won't help much at night, and 2, it harms the security of the containment.

Hmm… I considered an independent generator, but this seemed a more cost-effective solution. It ought to still help at night because there's always going to be at least some light.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an SCP I have always expected that would happen someday. I've always wondered whether the real reason why I couldn't pass through a mirror was because my double was in the way.

Anyway, upvote. Great first article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are several instances where the tone isn't clinical, such as:

+ show block
– hide block

but in fact encounters

expels these substances

not too sure if "these" refers to the gases that entered or if it was used to just refer to some other gas that it expels.

SCP-2645 is some form of

May just be a personal preference for this one, but "some" sounds pretty out of place for me, perhaps replace it with "a"?

Overall, I dislike the concept and it doesn't execute it well enough or put enough of a twist on it for me to like it. Not much you can do about the concept (for obvious reasons). And I can't suggest much to help the execution either, as I wouldn't know what or how to do that, but so be it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems like a kind of cliche idea, but you really ran with it and made it interesting, so it's a +1 from me.

Couple of small notes though,

Analysis of footage revealed that the measurement appearing on the reflected version of the thermocouple was at the expected temperature, not matching its counterpart in reality.

It's unclear what you mean by "expected". Do you mean it matched the real thermocouple, or it matched the temperature of the flames it produced?

Also, the containment procedures seem a bit unlikely. Surely an independent generator is preferable to putting it in a room with a skylight? 1, because skylights won't help much at night, and 2, it harms the security of the containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a rad interpretation of a simple concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much to everyone who took a look at this in the drafting stage, you were a great help!

Put a lot of work into this one, so I hope it paid off.
